title: Alsine meyeri
taxonomy: Alsine meyeri is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. It belongs to the genus Alsine, a group of flowering plants that are typically found in alpine and subalpine regions. The species Alsine meyeri is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. This species is closely related to other members of the genus Alsine, such as Alsine alpina and Alsine petraea. Alsine meyeri is a small, perennial herb with delicate white flowers that bloom in the summer months.

intro: Alsine meyeri, also known as Alsine or Alsine meyeri, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae) that grows in high-altitude alpine and subalpine regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Alsine meyeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Alsine meyeri is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems.
